home.meta_title: Solar Fence & SkyWind – Modern Energy Solutions
home.meta_description: Solar fences, SkyWind turbines and combo solutions for private, commercial and agricultural use. Discover and order now.
home.hero_sub: Premium solar installations and small wind turbines for private, commercial and agricultural use – order directly or configure.

benefits.title: Double use of space
benefits.desc: Fence, roof or meadow become an energy source – without losing space.

benefits.title: Individually planned
benefits.desc: No off-the-shelf product – tailored to your space and your goals.

benefits.title: Expert support
benefits.desc: By your side from the first idea to commissioning.

benefits.title: Future-proof
benefits.desc: Your own electricity from your own land – more independent from the energy market.

faq_items.q: Who is a solar fence suitable for?
faq_items.a: For anyone who needs a fence and wants to generate electricity at the same time. Especially suitable for private properties, commercial premises and agricultural areas.

faq_items.q: How much electricity does a SkyWind turbine produce?
faq_items.a: This depends on location, wind conditions and system size. In our consultation we jointly determine the realistic annual output based on your site data.

faq_items.q: Can solar and wind be combined?
faq_items.a: Yes – our combo solutions are designed exactly for this. Solar and wind complement each other ideally: when the sun is not shining, the wind often blows.
First, we speak about your project and your property without any obligation. On this basis, we create a tailored concept – without standard offers and without pressure.

faq_items.q: Are the products eligible for subsidies?
faq_items.a: Depending on the state, type of installation and use, various funding options are available. We will inform you about current programmes during the consultation.

home.cta_sub: Tell us your idea. We will show you the right solution – individually and without generic promises.

skywind.meta_title: SkyWind – Small Wind Turbines for Private and Commercial Sites
skywind.meta_desc: SkyWind small wind turbines generate electricity even when the sun is not shining. Compact, quiet and designed for individual sites.
skywind.sub: SkyWind small wind turbines complement solar systems or stand alone. Compact, quiet and suitable for private and commercial sites – where wind conditions are right.

highlights.title: Wind as a complement to solar
highlights.desc: Solar and wind complement each other across seasons and times of day: when cloudy skies reduce solar yields, wind often blows. The combination significantly increases energy security.

highlights.title: Compact design
highlights.desc: SkyWind systems are designed for properties that don't have space for large wind turbines. They fit private gardens, business premises and agricultural land.

highlights.title: Productive year-round
highlights.desc: Even in winter months when solar yields are lower, wind can contribute to self-supply. SkyWind closes this seasonal gap.

highlights.title: Site-based planning
highlights.desc: Wind conditions at your site are decisive. We analyse them before proposing a concept. A SkyWind system is only recommended where it makes economic sense.

skywind.usecases_sub: SkyWind is an individual solution – not a mass product. Whether it's worthwhile at your site, we'll work out together.

usecases.label: Private Home & Property
usecases.desc: As a complement to an existing solar installation or as a standalone solution. Suitable for properties with sufficient wind potential and structural suitability.
scenarios: Complement to a rooftop PV system
Complement to a solar fence
Standalone wind power supply

usecases.label: Commercial & Business
usecases.desc: For business premises, logistics areas and industrial sites with a high self-consumption share and open areas with usable wind resource.
scenarios: Power for operational needs
Combination with photovoltaics
Visible commitment to renewable energy

usecases.label: Agriculture & Farms
usecases.desc: Open agricultural areas often offer good wind conditions. SkyWind can be used here standalone or as part of a combined system.
scenarios: Self-supply for stables and farm buildings
Combination with solar fence or open-area PV
Use of open sites with good wind resource

skywind.trust_h2_line1: SkyWind is not the right choice
skywind.trust_p1: A small wind turbine only makes sense where sufficient and reliable wind blows. We only recommend SkyWind when the site analysis shows economically viable generation.
skywind.trust_p2: This means: before any offer, we always assess your specific site. If SkyWind doesn't make sense there, we say so – and suggest an alternative.

process_steps.title: Site assessment
process_steps.text: We analyse the wind conditions at your site based on your location and surrounding conditions.

process_steps.title: Economic viability assessment
process_steps.text: Based on the site data, we give you an honest assessment of whether and to what extent a system is worthwhile.

process_steps.title: Individual concept
process_steps.text: If SkyWind makes sense, you receive a tailored planning proposal – without standard offers.

faq_items.q: Which sites are suitable for SkyWind?
faq_items.a: SkyWind is suitable for sites with sufficient and reliable wind resource. Open locations, coastal areas or elevated properties are generally more favourable than sheltered inner-city locations. In our consultation we assess your site specifically before giving a recommendation.

faq_items.q: How loud is a small wind turbine?
faq_items.a: Modern small wind turbines are significantly quieter than older generations. The actual noise level depends on the type of system, wind speed and distance to residential or sleeping areas. We take this into account in site planning.

faq_items.q: Can SkyWind be combined with a solar system?
faq_items.a: Yes – this is one of the most common configurations. Solar and wind complement each other sensibly by season and time of day. The combination increases the self-supply share and reduces dependence on a single energy source. Details can be found on our combo solutions page.

faq_items.q: Do I need planning permission for a small wind turbine?
faq_items.a: This depends on the height of the system, the site and local regulations. In some municipalities planning permission is required, in others not. We support you in clarifying the relevant requirements at an early stage.

skywind.cta_sub: Tell us about your site and your goals. We'll assess the wind potential and give you an honest assessment within two working days.

solarzaun.meta_title: Solar Fence – Fencing and Energy in One
solarzaun.meta_desc: The solar fence combines property fencing with electricity generation – without additional space requirements. For private properties, commercial premises and agriculture.
solarzaun.sub: The solar fence combines the function of a fence with the electricity generation of a photovoltaic system – without additional space on your property. A solution for private, commercial and agricultural use.
solarzaun.highlights_h2: What makes the solar fence stand out.

highlights.title: Dual use without space loss
highlights.desc: The fence area becomes an energy source. No additional space is required – fencing and electricity generation share the same area.

highlights.title: Architecturally integrable
highlights.desc: The solar fence can be designed into the property. It suits modern new builds as well as existing installations.

highlights.title: Scalable to fence length
highlights.desc: Whether 20 or 200 metres of fence line: the system is designed for your specific situation. There is no standard size – only the solution for your property.

highlights.title: Individually planned
highlights.desc: Orientation, tilt, module selection and feed-in variant are calculated site-specifically. No two solar fences are the same.

solarzaun.usecases_h2: Who is a solar fence suitable for?
solarzaun.usecases_sub: Wherever fencing is already planned or in place, a solar fence can be usefully deployed.

usecases.label: Private Properties
usecases.desc: Homes with gardens, property fencing, privacy screens. The solar fence replaces or supplements a conventional fence – while generating electricity.
scenarios: Fencing the property
Privacy screen to road or neighbouring properties
Complement to an existing rooftop PV system

usecases.label: Commercial Premises
usecases.desc: Business sites, car parks, logistics centres. Fencing and energy generation can be economically combined.
scenarios: Fencing of business premises
Car park fencing with electricity generation
Security and energy use in one

usecases.label: Agriculture & Farms
usecases.desc: Large open spaces with long fence lines offer particularly high energy potential. The solar fence is suitable as a paddock fence, pasture enclosure or land boundary.
scenarios: Paddock and pasture fencing
Land boundary with electricity generation
High self-supply share through long fence length

solarzaun.trust_h2: Every solar fence is an individual project.
solarzaun.trust_p1: A solar fence is not an off-the-shelf product. Every fence line is different – in length, orientation, terrain profile and structural requirements. That is why every collaboration begins with a careful assessment.
solarzaun.trust_p2: We clarify with you: What is the realistic electricity generation at your site? Which module variant suits your fencing situation? How is feed-in or self-consumption handled? Only when these questions are answered does a meaningful concept emerge.

process_steps.title: Initial consultation
process_steps.text: We assess your property, your goals and the technical requirements.

process_steps.title: Concept
process_steps.text: Based on your situation, we create a tailored planning proposal.

process_steps.title: Implementation
process_steps.text: From planning to commissioning – we accompany the project.

solarzaun.faq_h2: Frequently asked questions about solar fences.

faq_items.q: What exactly is a solar fence?
faq_items.a: A solar fence is a fencing solution in which photovoltaic modules are integrated into the fence structure. It fulfils the function of a conventional fence – boundary, privacy, security – and generates electricity at the same time. Depending on the design, the electricity can be fed into the grid or used directly.

faq_items.q: Which properties are suitable for a solar fence?
faq_items.a: Basically any property that needs a fence. A solar fence is particularly advantageous with long fence lines and a favourable orientation. In a no-obligation initial consultation we clarify whether and how a solar fence is worthwhile at your site.

faq_items.q: Do I need planning permission for a solar fence?
faq_items.a: This depends on your municipality and local building regulations. In many cases no separate approval process is required – we support you in clarifying the relevant requirements in advance.

faq_items.q: How does a solar fence differ from a rooftop PV system?
faq_items.a: The solar fence uses the fence area instead of the roof. This is particularly interesting when the roof is already occupied, unfavourably oriented or unavailable. Both systems can also be combined.

solarzaun.cta_sub: Tell us about your property situation and your needs. We will get back to you within two working days with an initial concept proposal – without obligation and without standard offers.

kombiloesungen.meta_title: Combined Solutions – Solar and Wind. Working Together.
kombiloesungen.meta_desc: Maximum self-sufficiency through the interplay of solar fence, photovoltaics, storage and wind power. Individual system planning from a single source.
kombiloesungen.sub: Maximum self-sufficiency through the interplay of solar fence, photovoltaics, storage and wind power. We plan your system as a whole – no single solutions, no piecemeal approach.
kombiloesungen.system_sub: A combined solution is not a product – it's a coordinated system. Depending on location, available area and goals, we select the right components.

system_blocks.label: Solar Fence
system_blocks.description: Fencing and electricity generation in one. The solar fence forms the solar base in many projects – space-neutral and architecturally integrable.

system_blocks.label: Photovoltaics
system_blocks.description: Rooftop, open-area PV or facade integration supplement the solar fence and increase the overall solar generation output.

system_blocks.label: SkyWind
system_blocks.description: Power even when solar yields are low. Wind and solar complement each other by season and time of day – a combination that stabilises self-supply.

system_blocks.label: Storage
system_blocks.description: Self-generated electricity that is not immediately consumed can be stored and used on demand – during the day, at night or at peak loads.

kombiloesungen.usecases_h2: Who benefits from a combined solution?
kombiloesungen.usecases_sub: Wherever maximum self-sufficiency and multiple available energy sources come together.

usecases.label: Private Home with High Supply Needs
usecases.description: Those who want to be as independent from the grid as possible benefit from combining multiple energy sources. Solar fence, rooftop PV and storage can significantly increase the self-supply share.
scenarios: Solar fence as a space-neutral solar source
Storage for self-consumption around the clock
Optional: SkyWind for windy sites

usecases.label: Agriculture & Farms
usecases.description: Large open areas and long fencing requirements make agricultural operations ideal candidates for comprehensive combined solutions with a high self-supply share.
scenarios: Solar fence along paddocks and enclosures
Wind power on open areas
Storage for consistent supply

usecases.label: Commercial & Business
usecases.description: Commercial users with high self-supply needs benefit especially from systems that combine multiple generation sources and permanently reduce energy costs.
scenarios: Fencing and solar installation in one
Absorbing peak loads through own production
Independence from grid price fluctuations

kombiloesungen.trust_h2: No combined solution is like another.
kombiloesungen.trust_p1: Which components make sense depends on your location, available area, energy needs and budget. We don't recommend standard configurations – we analyse your specific case and put together a system that truly fits.
kombiloesungen.trust_p2: This also means: sometimes a single solution makes more sense than a combination. We tell you what actually makes sense for your situation – without over- or under-sizing.

process_steps.title: Situation analysis
process_steps.text: Location, area, energy needs and structural requirements are assessed together.

process_steps.title: System concept
process_steps.text: We develop a coordinated combination of the components that make sense for you.

process_steps.title: Planning & implementation
process_steps.text: From detailed planning to commissioning, we accompany the project as a whole.

faq_items.q: What components are part of a combined solution?
faq_items.a: A combined solution can include a solar fence, rooftop or open-area photovoltaics, a SkyWind small wind turbine and electricity storage – depending on what makes sense at your site. Not every project needs all components. We plan the system based on your situation.

faq_items.q: Is a combined solution worthwhile for my property?
faq_items.a: This depends on your available area, energy needs and local conditions. A combined solution is particularly worthwhile where multiple energy sources are available. In our consultation we analyse your specific case.

faq_items.q: How does planning a combined solution work?
faq_items.a: First, we assess your location, area and energy needs. On this basis we develop a system concept combining the most suitable components. Only then follows a concrete planning and cost proposal – without standard promises.

faq_items.q: Can I build a combined solution step by step?
faq_items.a: Yes – many projects start with one element, e.g. the solar fence, and are later expanded with storage or wind power. It is important that the first component is designed so that a later expansion remains technically possible.

kombiloesungen.cta_sub: Tell us about your property, your needs and your goals. We'll analyse which combination makes sense for you – without preconceptions and without standard offers.

privatkunden.meta_title: For Private Customers – Solar Fence, SkyWind and Combined Solutions
privatkunden.meta_desc: Your own electricity from your own property. Solar fence, SkyWind and combined solutions for homeowners, builders and households with self-supply ambitions.
privatkunden.sub: Solar fence, SkyWind and combined solutions for homeowners and builders. No off-the-shelf product – but a solution that fits your property, your home and your daily life.
privatkunden.profile_sub: Our private customers usually have one thing in common: they are not looking for an off-the-shelf solution, but something that truly fits their property and their goals.

profiles.label: Homeowners
profiles.description: You have a house with a property and are wondering what is possible with the fence line or garden. A solar fence uses the area that is going to be fenced anyway – and generates electricity you can use yourself.
fit: Interest in self-generation without additional space
Complement to an existing or planned rooftop PV system

profiles.label: Builders & Renovators
profiles.description: When you are building or renovating, it is the best moment to plan an energy solution from the start. Solar fence, wind and storage can be integrated in the planning phase much more easily than afterwards.
fit: New build: energy concept from scratch

profiles.label: Self-sufficiency seekers
profiles.description: You want to be as independent from the grid as possible – not at any cost, but as far as it makes sense. Combined solutions of solar, wind and storage significantly increase the self-supply share.
fit: Interest in combining multiple energy sources

scenarios.title: Solar fence as garden enclosure
scenarios.description: You are planning a new fence or want to replace the old one. Instead of a conventional fence, you integrate solar modules into the structure. The fence does what fences do – and generates electricity for your home or garage.

scenarios.title: Wind power as a complement to rooftop PV
scenarios.description: Your rooftop system produces well in summer, but little in winter. A SkyWind turbine can compensate for this – if the wind conditions at your site allow. Whether that is the case, we clarify in the consultation.

scenarios.title: New build: plan energy from the start
scenarios.description: When building new, you have the unique opportunity to coordinate fencing, solar and storage from the very beginning. This is cheaper, cleaner and more efficient than a subsequent addition.

scenarios.title: Combined solution for more self-supply
scenarios.description: Solar and wind complement each other by season and time of day. With storage, you can use self-generated electricity when it is needed. A combined solution significantly increases the self-supply share.

privatkunden.solutions_sub: All three solutions can be used individually or in combination. Which one fits your property and your goals, we work out together.

solutions.label: Solar Fence
solutions.description: Fencing and electricity generation in one. For gardens, properties and homes with existing or planned fencing needs.

solutions.label: SkyWind
solutions.description: Wind power as a complement to solar. For properties with sufficient wind resource – we assess your site honestly.

solutions.label: Combined Solutions
solutions.description: Coordinated systems of solar, wind and storage. For households that want to generate as much of their own electricity as possible.

privatkunden.process_p1: No sales pitch, no pre-prepared offers off the shelf. We listen first – and then ask the questions needed to develop a sensible solution.
privatkunden.process_p2: The initial consultation is free of charge and without obligation. You decide whether and when you want to proceed.

process_steps.title: Submit enquiry
process_steps.description: Tell us about your property, your idea and what you have in mind. Not a form for the drawer – we read every enquiry personally.

process_steps.title: First conversation
process_steps.description: We get back to you within two working days. Together we clarify whether and how a solution fits your situation – without pressure, without standard offers.

process_steps.title: Individual concept
process_steps.description: If it makes sense, we prepare an initial concept proposal tailored to your property, your needs and your budget.

trust_items: No purchase pressure, no follow-up calls
privatkunden.cta_sub: A brief picture of your situation is enough to start. We get back to you within two working days – personally, without a standard response and without obligation.

gewerbe_b2b.meta_title: Commercial & B2B – Energy Solutions for Your Project
gewerbe_b2b.meta_desc: Solar fence, SkyWind and combined solutions for commercial, agricultural and project development. Individual planning, scalable systems, collaborative partnerships.
gewerbe_b2b.sub: Solar fence, SkyWind and combined solutions for commercial, agricultural and project development. We work project-based – from the first enquiry to commissioning, scalable and tailored to your requirements.
gewerbe_b2b.target_h2: Which customers and partners we address.
gewerbe_b2b.target_sub: This page is aimed at commercial clients, agricultural businesses, project developers and potential partners. What they all have in common: individual requirements that don't allow for standard solutions.

target_groups.label: Commercial & Industrial
target_groups.description: Business premises, production areas, car parks, logistics centres. Where fencing is already planned, a solar fence can be economically and functionally integrated. Own electricity permanently reduces operating costs.
fit: Car park and storage area security
Combination with rooftop PV or wind power

target_groups.label: Agriculture & Farms
target_groups.description: Long fence lines, large open areas, exposed locations – agricultural businesses are predestined for solar fences and SkyWind. High self-supply share for stables, machinery and farm buildings.
fit: Paddock and pasture fencing with solar modules
Wind power for energy-intensive operating areas

target_groups.label: Project Developers & Investors
target_groups.description: Multiple properties, recurring requirements, long-term planning. We work project-based and scalably – with consistent quality across multiple sites.
fit: Planning at an early project stage

target_groups.label: Dealers & Installation Partners
target_groups.description: Resellers, solar installers and installation businesses who want to integrate our portfolio into their offering. We seek partnerships on equal terms – with clear conditions and shared project responsibility.

scenarios.title: Solar fence as business perimeter fencing
scenarios.description: Where fencing is planned anyway, the solar fence offers an economically sensible extension: the fence structure becomes an energy installation – without additional space requirements. For business premises, car parks and logistics areas with a relevant fence line.

scenarios.title: Combo system for energy-intensive businesses
scenarios.description: Production and storage businesses with high self-supply needs benefit from combined systems of solar fence, rooftop PV and storage. This reduces dependence on grid electricity and stabilises energy costs long-term.

scenarios.title: Large-scale agricultural installations
scenarios.description: Farms with long paddock and pasture fences have high energy potential. Solar fence along existing enclosures and SkyWind on open areas can together cover a significant share of operational electricity needs.

scenarios.title: Multi-site projects
scenarios.description: Project developers and investors with multiple properties benefit from consistent planning and execution. We document requirements, concepts and project progress systematically – for reproducible quality across sites.

gewerbe_b2b.solutions_h2: Three product lines for commercial projects.
gewerbe_b2b.solutions_sub: Each solution can be used individually or in combination. Which configuration makes sense for your project, we work out together.

solutions.label: Solar Fence
solutions.description: Fencing and electricity generation in one. Particularly efficient with long fence lines on commercial and agricultural land.

solutions.label: SkyWind
solutions.description: Wind power as a complement to solar – for sites with good wind conditions. Compact and scalable for commercial areas.

solutions.label: Combined Solutions
solutions.description: Coordinated systems of solar, wind and storage. The right approach for businesses with high self-supply ambitions.

gewerbe_b2b.process_p1: Commercial projects have different requirements from private individual projects. Larger areas, multiple decision-makers, tight timelines, documentation requirements. We align our project work accordingly.
gewerbe_b2b.process_p2: No project begins with an offer. It begins with a conversation – and a shared understanding of your situation.

process_steps.title: Initial enquiry & project description
process_steps.description: Tell us about your project: location, area, fence line, energy needs, timeline. The more we know in advance, the more targeted our response.

process_steps.title: Situation analysis & site assessment
process_steps.description: We assess the framework conditions – structural, technical, regulatory. On this basis we give you an honest assessment before moving to the concept phase.

process_steps.title: System concept & planning
process_steps.description: Based on the analysis we develop a tailored concept. No standard configuration – but the solution that fits your project and your goals.

process_steps.title: Implementation & commissioning
process_steps.description: From detailed planning to acceptance, we accompany the project. Documented, on schedule and with clear communication at all levels.

gewerbe_b2b.partner_p1: We work with solar installers, installation businesses and dealers who want to integrate our portfolio into their offering. No exclusivity contracts, no volume pressure – but a collaborative partnership with clear conditions and shared project responsibility.
gewerbe_b2b.partner_p2: If you regularly work with customers who are planning fencing or want to generate their own electricity, a partnership could be beneficial for both sides.

partner_cards.title: Product expansion
partner_cards.text: Solar fence, SkyWind and combined solutions as an addition to your existing portfolio.

partner_cards.title: Joint projects
partner_cards.text: We plan, you implement – or vice versa. Coordination based on your capacity.

partner_cards.title: Clear terms
partner_cards.text: Transparent partnership structure. No hidden requirements.

gewerbe_b2b.cta_sub: Tell us about your project. We will get back to you within two working days with an initial assessment – without standard offers and without obligation.
